greeting: Well hello there, fellow digital artisans!
introParagraph: Another week, another wave of plugin updates has crashed upon our shores! This week, we saw a modest but mighty collection of releases, proving that even a quieter week can deliver some significant punches, especially for those who love efficiency and robust site management. From command-line wizardry to critical SEO alerts, it's clear developers are focusing on empowering site owners with more control and smarter diagnostics.

highlights.title: WP Mail SMTP 4.9.0
highlights.summary: WP Mail SMTP just dropped a game-changer for agencies and dev teams with new WP-CLI commands! Now you can configure mailer settings, including SMTP and API-key setups, directly from the command line, making automated deployments and server management a breeze.

highlights.title: All in One SEO 4.9.9
highlights.summary: AIOSEO is stepping up its proactive game with a new 'SEO Alerts' feature, notifying site owners of critical SEO issues via email and Slack. This release also adds handy new smart tags for event dates, ensuring your structured data is always on point.

highlights.title: WPForms Lite 1.10.2.1
highlights.summary: WPForms squashed an annoying bug where forms scrolled back to the first input when adjusting a Number Slider on Chrome for Android. Plus, they've added an AI MCP settings section, hinting at future innovations in form management.

highlights.title: Uncanny Automator 7.3.1.4
highlights.summary: Uncanny Automator continues to fortify its integrations, hardening trigger token handling for several popular plugins like WP Event Manager, FluentCRM, and Gravity Forms against PHP Object Injection. This release is all about making your automations more secure and robust.

otherReleases.title: PushEngage 4.2.7
otherReleases.shortSummary: Improved: Web SDK script URL now includes the plugin version as a query param...

otherReleases.title: Thrive Themes 10.9.2.1
otherReleases.shortSummary: Thrive Leads: Refresh From Cloud: A new “Refresh from cloud” butt...

otherReleases.title: PushEngage 4.2.6
otherReleases.shortSummary: Improved: Strengthened plugin security across onboarding, WhatsApp, and crede...

closingJoke: Why did the plugin cross the road? To get to the other API! (And probably to fetch some new data, securely of course.)
